message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-utmy6 (TR/TC_DAG iteration order)

Fixed in commit 00b6dad.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -94,7 +94,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-turq0","title":"parity: node_connectivity/edge_connectivity silently return 0 on empty graph, nx raises NetworkXPointlessConcept","description":"fnx.node_connectivity(fnx.empty_graph(0)) returns 0; nx.node_connectivity(nx.empty_graph(0)) raises NetworkXPointlessConcept('Connectivity is undefined for the null graph.'). Same drift in edge_connectivity. Drop-in code that catches NetworkXPointlessConcept to gate empty-graph paths fails to trigger on fnx — silent value masks the contract violation. Fix: add the raise at the top of each wrapper, matching nx's documented behaviour. Single-node case (returns 0 on both) is unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:17:28.519121260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T07:19:20.086837509Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T07:19:20.084994560Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit c633959. node_connectivity/edge_connectivity raise NetworkXPointlessConcept on null graph matching nx. 8 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-ugod2","title":"parity: 4 branching/arborescence functions over-restrict undirected (or use wrong error wording)","description":"Four functions diverge from nx on undirected input: (1) maximum_branching raises NotImplemented; nx.maximum_branching(undirected) returns a maximum-spanning Graph result. (2) minimum_branching raises NotImplemented; nx returns an empty Graph (lowest sum is no edges). (3) maximum_spanning_arborescence and (4) minimum_spanning_arborescence raise with custom message '<name> is only implemented for directed graphs.' instead of nx's standard 'not implemented for undirected type'. Drop-in code that uses these on undirected breaks or fails to match nx's exact NotImplemented message. Fix: delegate branching variants to nx for undirected; align arborescence messages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:57:01.883605639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:23.989928755Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:59:23.987956927Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit f0d25ef. min/maximum_branching now accept undirected (delegate to nx); min/maximum_spanning_arborescence raise nx's standard NotImplemented message. 10 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-upl6v","title":"parity: VF2 Matcher classes shadowed node_match/edge_match callbacks (TypeError on kwarg)","description":"_matcher_factory at __init__.py:25532 declared __init__(self, G1, G2) — accepting only G1, G2. The earlier inline GraphMatcher/DiGraphMatcher/MultiGraphMatcher/MultiDiGraphMatcher classes (line 7397) had the right signature with node_match/edge_match, but the factory at line 25556 overwrites them via module-level reassignment. Drop-in code calling fnx.GraphMatcher(G1, G2, node_match=fn) hit TypeError. Fix: update factory __init__ to accept and forward node_match=None, edge_match=None to nx's parent ctor. 12 parity tests freeze the contract: signature exact match for all 4 classes, callback semantics for node_match (always-false rejects iso), edge_match colour match on DiGraphMatcher (mismatched colours reject), kwarg acceptance on Multi variants, default 2-arg form still works.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:46:32.989586489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:47:14.438752039Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:47:14.436897428Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 36851b9. _matcher_factory now accepts node_match=None, edge_match=None and forwards them to nx's parent ctor. inspect.signature exact-matches nx for all 4 Matcher classes. 12 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-utmy6","title":"transitive_reduction / transitive_closure_dag adj + edge order drift from nx","description":"fnx.transitive_reduction and fnx.transitive_closure_dag both use the Rust binding which produces output graphs whose edge iteration order and per-node adj iteration order drift from nx.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('a','c'),('b','d'),('c','d'),('d','e')]
  fnx.transitive_reduction(dag).edges() -> [('a','c'),('a','b'),('b','d'),('c','d'),('d','e')]
  nx.transitive_reduction(dagx).edges() -> [('a','b'),('a','c'),('b','d'),('c','d'),('d','e')]

  fnx.transitive_closure_dag(dag).adj['a'] -> ['b','c','d','e']
  nx.transitive_closure_dag(dagx).adj['a']  -> ['b','c','e','d']
  (the new transitive edge to 'e' is appended in nx's discovery order, which is BFS-from-d)

Drop-in code that iterated R.edges() or adj[node] of either output silently broke.

Fix: delegate both to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors the cycle_basis/find_cycle/strongly_connected_components/intersection delegation pattern).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:39:20.232090108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:40:09.227372084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-utmy6","title":"transitive_reduction / transitive_closure_dag adj + edge order drift from nx","description":"fnx.transitive_reduction and fnx.transitive_closure_dag both use the Rust binding which produces output graphs whose edge iteration order and per-node adj iteration order drift from nx.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('a','c'),('b','d'),('c','d'),('d','e')]
  fnx.transitive_reduction(dag).edges() -> [('a','c'),('a','b'),('b','d'),('c','d'),('d','e')]
  nx.transitive_reduction(dagx).edges() -> [('a','b'),('a','c'),('b','d'),('c','d'),('d','e')]

  fnx.transitive_closure_dag(dag).adj['a'] -> ['b','c','d','e']
  nx.transitive_closure_dag(dagx).adj['a']  -> ['b','c','e','d']
  (the new transitive edge to 'e' is appended in nx's discovery order, which is BFS-from-d)

Drop-in code that iterated R.edges() or adj[node] of either output silently broke.

Fix: delegate both to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity (mirrors the cycle_basis/find_cycle/strongly_connected_components/intersection delegation pattern).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T15:39:20.232090108Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T15:43:12.280621211Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T15:43:12.278582009Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-uuo38","title":"parity: classes.filters hide_*/show_*/no_filter wrappers (11 fns)","description":"networkx.classes.filters exposes 11 filter factory functions used to construct subgraph views: hide_nodes(nodes), hide_edges(edges), hide_diedges(edges), hide_multiedges(edges), hide_multidiedges(edges), show_nodes(nodes), show_edges(edges), show_diedges(edges), show_multiedges(edges), show_multidiedges(edges), no_filter(*items). franken_networkx does not surface these. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__. Each returns a filter callable used by subgraph_view.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T22:49:38.907487801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:04:39.857592364Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:04:39.855657558Z","close_reason":"Shipped in de44f96: 11 filter factory wrappers (hide_/show_ variants for nodes/edges/diedges/multiedges/multidiedges, plus no_filter). Verified including end-to-end subgraph_view(P5, filter_node=hide_nodes([2])) -> [0,1,3,4].","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-v0ryp","title":"parity: maybe_regular_expander missing create_using/max_tries kwargs","description":"fnx.maybe_regular_expander(n, d, seed=None) lacked nx's keyword-only create_using=None and max_tries=100. Drop-in callers passing either kwarg hit TypeError. Aligned signature so the alias matches the longer maybe_regular_expander_graph and nx exactly. 5 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T03:56:47.373364568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:57:28.654673710Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:57:28.651329239Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit a210c20. maybe_regular_expander now accepts keyword-only create_using=None and max_tries=100, forwarding to maybe_regular_expander_graph. 5 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-v9m7b","title":"parity: triadic_census dict iterates in arbitrary Rust order, nx uses canonical 16-triad-type order","description":"fnx.triadic_census returns a dict with the 16 triad types in arbitrary Rust internal order (e.g. ['120U', '300', '012', '111D', ...]). nx returns them in the canonical MAN-notation order ['003', '012', '102', '021D', '021U', '021C', '111D', '111U', '030T', '030C', '201', '120D', '120U', '120C', '210', '300']. Drop-in code that iterates 'for triad_type, count in census.items(): ...' (or saves the dict in a JSON/CSV in canonical order) breaks. Fix: reorder result via the existing _TRIAD_TYPES constant which is already in canonical order.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:23:30.454400037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:25:34.743757311Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:25:34.741701377Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 028ea58. triadic_census now returns dict in canonical MAN-notation order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
